findings.control_title: Ensure 'Secure transfer required' is set to 'Enabled'
findings.description: Storage accounts should require secure transfer (HTTPS only)
findings.rationale: HTTPS encrypts data in transit and prevents man-in-the-middle attacks
findings.remediation:
Enable secure transfer:
        1. Go to Azure Portal → Storage Accounts
        2. Select storage account
        3. Settings → Configuration
        4. Set 'Secure transfer required' to 'Enabled'

        Or use Azure CLI:
        az storage account update \
          --name <storage-account-name> \
          --resource-group <resource-group> \
          --https-only true

findings.description: Storage service encryption protects data at rest
findings.rationale: Encryption at rest protects data from unauthorized access
findings.remediation:
Storage encryption is enabled by default on all new storage accounts.
        For existing accounts without encryption, create a new account and migrate data.

findings.control_title: Ensure storage accounts disallow public access
findings.description: Anonymous public read access should be disabled
findings.rationale: Public access can expose sensitive data to unauthorized users
findings.remediation:
Disable public access:
        1. Azure Portal → Storage Account → Configuration
        2. Set 'Allow Blob public access' to 'Disabled'

        Or use Azure CLI:
        az storage account update \
          --name <storage-account-name> \
          --resource-group <resource-group> \
          --allow-blob-public-access false
